title: eriogonum butterworthianum
intro: Eriogonum butterworthianum, also known as Butterworth's buckwheat, is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family, Polygonaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high content of antioxidants and its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Eriogonum butterworthianum can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its leaves and stems can also be used to make a salve or ointment for topical application.
